title: Blood lead secular trend in a cohort of children in Mexico City (1987-2002).
ehr_evidence: The study used structured interviews, blood lead determinations, and environmental monitoring data rather than electronic health records.
summary: A longitudinal cohort study investigating the secular trend of blood lead concentrations in children in Mexico City from 1987 to 2002, while quantifying the relative contributions of various lead exposure sources.
key_findings: Determined secular trends in blood lead concentrations over a 10-year period.
Quantified the relative contribution of several lead sources to blood lead levels.
captured_features: Air lead concentrations from monitoring stations
Birth characteristics (Apgar score, birth weight, gestation age)
population: 321 children born between 1987 and 1992 in Mexico City
exposure_domain: Lead (air pollution, industrial sources, lead-glazed ceramics)
limitations: Difficulty in specifying and measuring different lead sources
Data only provide the lower limit of the contribution of each source
